prompt:
Data-platform ticket. Our nightly ETL orchestrator shells out to the Databricks CLI and greps its stdout — it double-submits runs whenever the network hiccups, and nobody can tell WHICH task in the run actually failed. Replace that with a proper Python package `dbxjobs` (stdlib only) that drives one-time job runs over the Databricks Jobs REST API and reports per-task results. test_databricks_run.py is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Databricks documentation for the Jobs API — at minimum the official reference/SDK documentation for submitting a one-time run (the current API version and exact path, the idempotency_token semantics and its length cap), getting a run and its status model (the modern status field with termination_details vs the deprecated state field), getting run output (including why a multi-task parent run has no output of its own and the output size cap), and cancelling a run (including whether cancellation is synchronous). Only first-party Databricks pages count as sources (docs.databricks.com, the official Databricks SDK docs, or Databricks' own SDK repos). Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use:
- dbxjobs.errors: DbxApiError with .status_code, .error_code, .message (decoded from Databricks' {error_code, message} envelope; str() surfaces the error_code; no exception text may ever contain the bearer token or Authorization value), and RateLimitError(DbxApiError) with .retry_after.
- dbxjobs.client.JobsClient(base_url, token, sleep=<injected>, max_retries=2, backoff_seconds=1.0): methods submit_run(...), get_run(run_id), get_run_output(run_id), cancel_run(run_id) hitting the documented Jobs API endpoints with Bearer auth, Accept: application/json everywhere and Content-Type: application/json on POSTs. 429 responses sleep exactly the Retry-After header seconds (via the injected sleep) and retry up to max_retries, then raise RateLimitError; 500/503 sleep backoff_seconds and retry the same way. Never time.sleep.
- dbxjobs.runner.OneTimeRunner(client, poll_interval=5).run(tasks, run_name=..., idempotency_token=None, cancel=None) -> report. It submits ONE one-time run (tasks passed through verbatim; if the caller gives no idempotency_token, generate one <=64 chars and reuse the exact same token on any submit retry — that's what makes retrying safe), then polls the run through the documented lifecycle states until terminal, sleeping poll_interval between polls. `cancel` is a zero-arg callable checked after each non-terminal poll: the first time it's truthy, POST the documented cancel operation exactly once, then KEEP POLLING — cancellation is asynchronous — until the run reaches its terminal state.
- The report exposes: run_id, life_cycle_state (final status state), result_state, termination (the termination_details dict verbatim), canceled (bool), task_outputs and task_errors keyed by task_key. After a non-canceled terminal run, fetch output once per TASK run_id from the run's tasks array, in task order — never with the parent run_id. An output whose body carries `error` lands in task_errors as {'error', 'error_trace'}; anything else lands in task_outputs verbatim. A canceled run collects no outputs.

Acceptance: python3 test_databricks_run.py — it spins up a loopback fake Databricks workspace; no real workspace, no real credentials. test_databricks_run.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Databricks Jobs REST API 2.2 (POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/submit and related run endpoints)

official_sources.title: w.jobs: Jobs — Databricks SDK for Python documentation
official_sources.used_for: submit() one-time run parameters (tasks, run_name, idempotency_token max 64 chars, timeout_seconds); get_run pagination params; get_run_output limited to the first 5 MB; cancel_run cancels asynchronously so the run may still be running when the call returns

official_sources.title: Updating from Jobs API 2.1 to 2.2 | Databricks on AWS
official_sources.used_for: Jobs API 2.2 is the current version; tasks arrays are capped at 100 elements with next_page_token replacing has_more; Get a single run supports pagination and only_latest

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-go service/jobs/impl.go (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: exact 2.2 REST paths and headers: POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/submit, GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get, GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get-output, POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/cancel; Accept: application/json on all, Content-Type: application/json on POSTs

official_sources.title: Jobs dataclasses — Databricks SDK for Python documentation
official_sources.used_for: Run object fields (run_id, status replacing deprecated state, tasks with per-task run_id/task_key/attempt_number, run_page_url, next_page_token); RunOutput fields (notebook_output.result/truncated, error, error_trace, logs, metadata); termination_details {code, type, message}

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java RunLifecycleStateV2State.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: status.state enum values: BLOCKED, PENDING, QUEUED, RUNNING, TERMINATED, TERMINATING, WAITING

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java TerminationCodeCode.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: termination codes pinned by tests: SUCCESS (completed successfully), RUN_EXECUTION_ERROR (completed with task failures), USER_CANCELED (successfully canceled during execution by a user)

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java RunResultState.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: legacy state.result_state enum: SUCCESS, FAILED, CANCELED, TIMEDOUT, SUCCESS_WITH_FAILURES, UPSTREAM_FAILED, UPSTREAM_CANCELED, EXCLUDED, DISABLED, MAXIMUM_CONCURRENT_RUNS_REACHED

research.language_rationale: The batch-orchestration tooling this runner slots into is Python. Databricks' official Python SDK is a thin generated wrapper over these same REST endpoints, and the verify environment is stdlib-only, so urllib.request against the documented Jobs API 2.2 endpoints is the credible, dependency-free integration surface; the REST reference is Databricks' primary documented interface.
